title: Identification Type
text: Identification Type Method used to identify the beginning of an included entities and occurrences using variable length techniques. Value Description None Variable length techniques not used. String Identifier Use an ASCII string to identify included entities and their occurrences. You can specify the respective strings using the Entity Identifier , First Occurrence Identifier , and Next Occurrence Identifier . x -byte binary length Binary length where x is a value from one through four. String Id. and x -byte binary length Use a combination of string identifiers and binary length. x -byte binary length and String-Id Use a combination of binary length and the string identifiers. Description You can choose to use identify included entities and their occurrences based on a specified string, the binary length, or a combination of the two. Choose the option that you want to use from the list provided. Applies to Modeled Entity Interface Related Links Data Storage Field interface definitions, as defined in the Database Interface property, determine how data is stored in a specific DBMS storage format, and how Uniface deals with the data when it is retrieved. The definitions specify the Uniface data type and packing code, which are mapped by the DBMS connector to the DBMS data type, known as a storage format. Entity Identifier ASCII string that marks the beginning of an included entity. First Occurrence Identifier ASCII string that marks the beginning of the first occurrence of an included entity. Next Occurrence Identifier ASCII string that marks the beginning of all subsequent occurrences of an included entity when using variable-length techniques. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
